Current rows allow merges on the bottom row (64,2,2,2) and no horizontal merges elsewhere; columns allow a merge only in the third column (8,2,8,2 has none) and fourth column (2,32,4,2 has none), but moving down/up can merge the bottom row's 2s after shifting vertically only if aligned—here the immediate guaranteed merge is horizontal on the bottom row. Goal is to preserve mobility and ideally keep the 128 tile stable while creating space. Left turns bottom row into [64,4,2,0], creating one empty cell and keeping larger tiles relatively ordered; right gives [0,64,2,4], which disrupts ordering more. Up and down appear to make no merges and may be invalid. So left is best.
